name: HIV - Low CD4 Count (AIDS Threshold) Query
description: Tests ability to find HIV/AIDS patients via low CD4 cell count. CD4 < 200 cells/μL defines AIDS regardless of opportunistic infections. CD4 monitoring guides ART decisions and prophylaxis (e.g., PJP prophylaxis at CD4 < 200). This test exercises a less-than threshold value-quantity filter on a cell-count Observation — a unique data type for the suite.
prompt: Find laboratory observations for CD4 cell counts where the value is below 200 cells per microliter, indicating AIDS or severe HIV-related immunosuppression.

prompts.naive: Find patients with low CD4 counts.
prompts.broad: Find Observation resources for CD4 helper T-cell counts where the value is less than 200 cells per microliter. CD4 < 200 cells/μL is the CDC threshold for AIDS diagnosis regardless of opportunistic infections. CD4 < 200 also triggers prophylaxis for opportunistic infections like Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia (PJP).
prompts.expert: I need to find HIV patients at the AIDS stage by searching Observation resources for low CD4 T-cell counts. Use the LOINC code for CD3-positive CD4-positive T-helper cells as an absolute count per volume in blood, and apply a value filter for counts strictly below two hundred cells per microliter — the CDC threshold that defines AIDS-stage HIV regardless of opportunistic infections. This cell-count observation query captures only the AIDS-stage subset of the HIV population; patients with well-controlled HIV on antiretroviral therapy typically have CD4 counts well above this threshold and will not be returned.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with low CD4 (Paths A and B for AIDS-stage HIV). Tests cell-count Observation queries — a unique data type. Distinguishes AIDS-stage HIV from controlled HIV (CD4 ≥ 500) and partially-controlled HIV (CD4 200-500).
